Name: B0015
Notes: Double terminator — B0010+B0012 in tandem. iGEM BioBrick standard. Tandem design ensures near-zero read-through.

Name: ECK120029600
Notes: High-efficiency intrinsic terminator from iGEM/CIDAR characterized library. Sequence-defined with quantified activity.

Name: T7
Notes: High efficiency but ONLY IN BACTERIA THAT HAVE T7 RNA polymerase. Not broadly portable — only use in T7 expression systems.

Name: amyE
Notes: Standard B. subtilis terminator. High efficiency intrinsic. amyE is also the primary integration locus so naturally flanks integration sites.

Name: pepN
Notes: High efficiency intrinsic terminator from nisin biosynthesis cluster. Standard for LAB expression.

Name: SV40_polyA
Notes: Most widely used mammalian terminator. Efficient cleavage and polyadenylation. Found in virtually every mammalian expression vector.

Name: BGH_polyA
Notes: Very efficient. Standard in pCDNA3.1. Interchangeable with SV40 poly-A in most contexts.

Name: hGH_polyA
Notes: Largest common mammalian poly-A but strongest in some cell types. Includes downstream enhancer elements.

Name: rbGlob_polyA
Notes: Compact and efficient. Preferred for size-limited AAV vectors. Often combined with WPRE.

Name: CYC1
Notes: Most widely used yeast terminator. Efficient 3' processing. Used with virtually every yeast expression vector.

Name: ADH1
Notes: High efficiency. Common alternative to CYC1. Preferred when CYC1 causes read-through.

Name: PGK1
Notes: Efficient 3' processing. Common in metabolic engineering cassettes.

Name: AOX1
Notes: Standard P. pastoris terminator. Found in all pPICZ and pHIL vectors.

Name: NOS
Notes: Most widely used plant terminator. Efficient in both dicots and monocots. Found in virtually every plant binary vector.

Name: OCS
Notes: Compact efficient alternative to NOS terminator in dicots.

Name: 35S_CaMV
Notes: Naturally paired with 35S promoter. Efficient polyadenylation in dicots.

Name: rbcS-E9
Notes: Efficient in both monocots and dicots. More broadly portable than NOS in some monocot contexts.

Name: trpC
Notes: Most widely used filamentous fungal terminator. Efficient 3' processing. Found in virtually every Aspergillus vector.
